L1.area: Firmware image decompression and data extraction logic

L2.description: A heap out-of-bounds write vulnerability exists in the EFI/Tiano decompression routine. A loop that populates a code-length array reads the iteration count from the compressed input stream without clamping it to the array's declared size, allowing a crafted firmware image to write past the end of the heap-allocated buffer. A separate run-length expansion path within the same routine also lacks an upper-bound check, enabling additional out-of-bounds zero writes. Exploitation can cause a deterministic crash or, depending on heap layout, may allow code execution.

L3.area: Tiano/EFI decompression engine and its Huffman code-length decoding routines
L3.description: A heap out-of-bounds write vulnerability exists in the Tiano/EFI decompression routine. When reading Huffman code lengths from a compressed data stream, a loop reads the iteration count from a bitfield in the input without clamping it to the declared size of the destination code-length array, allowing a crafted firmware image to write past the end of the heap-allocated buffer. A run-length expansion path within the same code-length reader also lacks an upper-bound check, enabling additional out-of-bounds zero fills. The project ships its own copy of the decompressor without upstream hardening fixes.

ground_truth.reason: The Tiano decompressor reads an iteration count from the compressed bitstream without clamping it to the destination array size, allowing a crafted firmware image to trigger a heap out-of-bounds write in the code-length population loops.
